title: Washing the Face
short_rule: Washing the face completely from the top of the forehead to the bottom of the chin, and from earlobe to earlobe, is obligatory.
details: Washing the face, whose lengthwise demarcation is from the top of the forehead to the bottom of the chin for someone without a thick beard, or to the bottom of the beard. Its demarcation in width is from one earlobe to the other.
remedy_or_consequence: If omitted, the ablution is invalid and must be repeated.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Wiping a Fourth of the Head
short_rule: It is an obligatory integral of Wudu to wipe at least one-fourth of the head.
details: Wiping a fourth of the head is one of the four obligatory integrals of wudu'. The demarcation of the head is that above the ears.
remedy_or_consequence: If omitted, the ablution is invalid.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Maintaining the Correct Order
short_rule: It is an emphasized sunnah to wash the limbs in the exact sequence mentioned in the Qur'an.
details: Maintaining the correct order of limbs, as Allah Most High has listed in His Book (face, arms, head, and feet), is an emphasized sunna. Performing wudu' in this order is not an obligation, yet one would have done wrong by leaving it.
remedy_or_consequence: Omission entails doing wrong (isa'a), but the wudu remains valid.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Facing the Qibla
short_rule: It is recommended etiquette to face the direction of the Ka'ba while performing ablution.
details: Facing the qibla (direction of the Ka'ba in Makka) is an etiquette (adab) of wudu'. Etiquette is synonymous with recommended act (mustahabb or mandub).
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Speaking During Ablution
short_rule: It is disliked to engage in worldly speech while performing ablution.
details: Speaking during wudu', other than making supplication, is a disliked action.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Seeking Physical Assistance
short_rule: Having someone else wash or wipe your limbs during wudu without a valid excuse is disliked.
details: Having someone else assist one in wudu' without a valid excuse is disliked. It is not disliked to seek assistance in bringing or pouring the water; rather, it is disliked to have someone else assist in the actual washing or wiping.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Touching the Qur'an
short_rule: It is prohibited to touch a physical copy of the Qur'an without Wudu.
details: A person in a state of minor ritual impurity is prohibited from touching a verse of the Qur'an, unless doing so with a nonattached barrier.
remedy_or_consequence: Must perform Wudu before touching the script or pages.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Wudu Before Sleeping
short_rule: It is recommended to renew or perform Wudu right before going to sleep.
details: Wudu is recommended (mandub) when a person is in a state of minor ritual impurity before sleeping, or after waking up from sleep.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Distance from Water Excuse
short_rule: Tayammum is permitted if you are approximately 1.86 km (1 Hashimite mile) away from a water source.
details: Tayammum is permissible when there is a valid excuse, such as being roughly one Hashimite legal mile [1.16 mi (1.86 km)] away from water, whether on a journey or within a city.
remedy_or_consequence: Can perform dry ablution to pray validly.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Striking the Earth
short_rule: You must strike the earth exactly twice: once for wiping the face and once for the arms.
details: When a Muslim intends to remove the state of ritual impurity, he strikes the earth once and wipes his face completely with dirt, strikes it a second time and wipes his arms completely. Both 'strikes' are a condition for a valid tayammum.
remedy_or_consequence: If either strike is missed, the tayammum is invalid.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali
